C[enjoycarrots: This is why I don't play anymore. Game's been ""over"" for a lot of moves now, but the time settings make it never end to the point I've been over trying to actually play the game for weeks now. Just trying to play the last moves to make the game end, and I lose a won game because of something ridiculously stupid.

kobuz: I wouldn't mind if you requested undo. I've been in your position too often myself (also in real life tournaments) so I kinda understand your frustration. But how do you define when the game is over? I could say the same thing about the position up to 100 moves where white was leading even more.

enjoycarrots: I didn't mean an attack on you personally, just how my games have gone and how much it frustrates me. It wasn't appropriate to vent that in your direction, since there's nothing wrong with just playing on even if you are behind. Regarding move 100 or so, unless there's a catastrophic mistake on the board I would rarely consider the game over at that point. The mistakes that might cause a 20 point swing over the course of the rest of the game could all be minor, and hoping to win at that point doesn't hinge on your opponent making some monumental blunder. I'd consider the game ""over"" at the point in which only a major unforced error from your opponent could reasonably switch the outcome. Again, though, there's nothing wrong with playing on even after that point, as long as you continue playing honest moves.
enjoycarrots: You're good. My frustrations are at why the game just isn't fun for me anymore, and you didn't cause them.

enjoycarrots: I meant ""over"" in the sense that the outcome is pretty set in stone, not in the sense that you were being rude by playing on. But, it definitely didn't come off that way, and I apologize!

C[ajiadjuster: I don't yet understand this kind of opening conceptually.  I have the option of choosing a joseki that allows me to keep sente so that I can respond as needed to both approaches and shape the opening to my advantage.  White clearly has other plans, so it will be interesting to see what happens.
ajiadjuster: White doesn't have to give me sente, of course, but then they will owe me a move, which can be very costly.
C[ajiadjuster: It feels like White came out ahead, which suggests my response at D6 wasn't a good choice.  At least I get sente assuming White needs to fix at D7.

C[ajiadjuster: White owes me a move around D7, but I want to see how the board develops before attacking there.  I needed to mitigate White's influence and considered F16, but K16 puts me in a more flexible position.
C[ajiadjuster: The moves around H4, M3 look strange to me.  White had all that influence and went for 15 points on the side instead?
